item.title: Star Trek: Section 31 - re:View
item.author: RedLetterMedia
item.content:encoded: The ultimate cosmic confusion has set in for these two Graveyard Grandpas™ as they watch the newest “Star Trek” “Movie” Star Trek: Section 31. A flop years in the making costing Paramount Plus hundreds of millions of dollars putting them nearly 900 billion dollars in debt. And also costing them the good will of zillions of fans (except for really really dumb ones) all for one man, Alex Trebek. So when Star Trek Discovery was first made. Michelle Yeoh plays a character called Phillip George. She was Bo Burnham’s Captain on the USS Shengie. Burnham screwed up. Georgie Plagu died. Then in the mirror verse, Shelly Burnham discovered Phillp's Georgous has a clone called Evil lady Empress. The leader of the evil Terran Umpire. That evil character would stick around and play games with yum yum girl, punching baddies and saying cool lines like, "wanna go make that man scream?" then I'm not sure what happened to her character? I think she few into Kurtzman’s space hole and went to the future. At some point she must have traveled back in time, but not all the way back because she owns a space station shaped like a birth control pill dispenser and is friends with Rachel Garrett from Yesterday's Enterprise. Like, she was her age like maybe 68 years after the Events in Discovery? I know Michelle Yeoh looks youthful, but c’mon! Not 147 years old! Either way, Alex Kurtzmop thought this character was so well like she gets her own show. He plan for it. Then the China virus plague happened and everyone in California had to hide in their homes and wear masks. No filming! Says someone! Then when that cleared up Yeoh wins an Oscar and can no longer be paid peanuts for playing space psychopath. Tell me about it! Show me the money! Put that money everywhere everything is all at once! She told Kurtzmop where he could stick his hot dog fingers. So because of contracts and cruelty Kurtzmop decided to make Yeoh play space witch one more time for Star Trek. What a disaster. Thank god this is just a "movie" and not 10 episodes of this trash. Saved me some time. What a gas! Flat unfunny characters. Lame plot. Meh effects. I've soiled diapers that look more stylish and cool than this crap. This makes Borderlands look like Rebel Moon. Now, these two Graveyard Grandpas™ are a little behind on their Trek cannon. Who is where and why and why? Does it matter to watch this? Not really. Is Phillip Georgo the main character that you're supposed to sympathize with? I think so? Not sure ? I think you're just supposed to think that this wacky group of unlikely heroes are cool for saving the universe from a super bomb. Why do I care about the universe at all? Seems kind of miserable, mean, dark, cruel, evil, cold, unfunny and has lousy visual FX.

item.title: When an actor is more than just funny - Jim Carrey
item.author: FilmStack
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Displate for sponsoring this video! Use our code FILMSTACK to get your Displates discounted or click on our link https://displate.com/@filmstack to get the discount automatically applied at checkout.
With Ace Ventura, The Mask and Dumb and Dumber all releasing within a single year, Jim Carrey was at the top of Hollywood. But for him it wasn't enough. Instead of playing it safer and only doing films like The Cable Guy and Bruce Almighty, Carrey wanted to prove he can do more than make people laugh by giving a go at more dramatic roles. No one would have ever thought that the slapstick king could star in films like The Truman Show, Man on the Moon, and Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind, but that's what he did.
But the transition into dramatic roles wasn't smooth, and he didn't really fit in a certain place in Hollywood. And as he got lost in his roles, he began to lose himself. So today, we’re exploring this transformative chapter of Jim Carrey’s career. What early struggles drove him to take such bold risks? How did he balance comedy and drama? And did his quest for more fulfill him? This is the story of when comedy wasn’t enough. This is Jim Carrey.
Chapters: 00:00 - Intro 02:34 - King of Comedy 07:27 - Leap Into Drama 12:19 - Uncertainty 16:00 - Legacy
Jim Carrey. Ace Ventura. The Mask. Cameron Diaz. Dumb and Dumber. Riddler. Batman Forever. The Cable Guy. Liar Liar. The Truman Show. Man on the Moon. The Majestic. How The Grinch Stole Christmas. Bruce Almighty. Me Myself and Irene. Bruce Almighty. Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind. The Number 23. Lemony Snicket. Yes Man. Sonic the Hedgehog. Dumb and Dumber To. Mr Popper's Penguins. The Incredible Burt Wonderstone. No Oscar Nominations. Golden Globe Jim Carrey.
If you like this video don't forget to leave a like, and if you're interested in videos about movies and the film industry in general, make sure to subscribe to FilmStack for more great content.
If you have any other ideas for videos, leave a comment and I might make a video with your idea.
Patreon: https://patreon.com/FilmStack Twitter: https://twitter.com/realFilmStack Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/realfilmstack/
#jimcarrey #comedyactor #bestactor

item.title: Impressions
item.author: RedLetterMedia
item.content:encoded: Mike hosts a new game show where Josh, Jay and Rich Evans all are challenged to attempt impressions they have no idea how to do. Mike has randomly selected the impressions for them to do for his personal amusement. Josh wonders how he got here and attempts to help Rich and Jay as they stumble through the challenges like perhaps children attempting to solve an advanced quantum mathematics problem presented by an MIT professor. It is a hopeless cause, but one worthy of laughing at their attempts right to their stupid faces. Will Josh win? Will Rich win? Will Jay win? Who knows! Also, there's a special bonus at the end of the video.

item.title: I hate this movie so much. - Munchie (1992)
item.author: FanboyFlicks
item.content:encoded:
On this episode of Weird Movies With Mark, I talk about Munchie (1992) and now that the video is done, I'm so glad that I'll never have to talk about it again.

item.title: THE SECOND | Animatic vs. Final (Bonus Video)
item.author: Every Frame a Painting
item.content:encoded:
A side-by-side comparison of the animatic for “The Second” and the final film, with commentary provided by Taylor Ramos & Tony Zhou. Please enjoy, and we’ll see you for the next project.
Storyboarded by Taylor Ramos Edited by Tony Zhou
Chapters 00:00 Introduction 01:08 Commentary Start, Realities of Production 02:09 Project Origins, Barry Lyndon, Creating Animatic 03:07 Working Process with Animatic 03:50 Green Screen PMP, 2nd Unit Reshoots 04:52 Zorro masks, long lenses, compromising with locations 06:07 Compressing multiple set-ups into one 06:59 Splitting up locations to break up movie visually 07:30 Duel negotiations 08:21 The Back to Back shot 09:01 Taylor’s plexiglass drawovers on set 09:33 Paul and Ethan in the argument scene 10:38 Composer, music cues 11:09 Tony’s composition vs. Taylor’s composition 11:31 Yes it was a real cloud passing his face 12:00 Painterly sequence, gun montage, VFX sky replacements 13:00 Subjectivity, no dialogue at beginning or end 13:50 Directing Ethan & Colton for the duel 14:40 Alfonso's moments of "ma," Taylor's first drawing 15:31 Deleted Shots & Scenes & Thank Yous
Links to some of the clips The Rescuers (1977) story session: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=flLu5iDVI7c Zootopia (2016) deleted scene: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MnH46nq8gao The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014) animatic clip: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3rcVFEgCaOM
You can check out some merch we made here: https://store.dftba.com/collections/every-frame-a-painting

item.title: THE SECOND | Short Film
item.author: Every Frame a Painting
item.content:encoded:
In an alternate version of today’s world where dueling is still acceptable, Philip, a man of tradition, must perform the role of “Second” on the day of his only son’s duel.
“The Second,” starring Paul Sun-Hyung Lee (Kim’s Convenience, The Mandalorian, Avatar: The Last Airbender) and Ethan Hwang (Riceboy Sleeps, Umbrella Academy) is the first live-action narrative short written & directed by Taylor Ramos and Tony Zhou. It premiered at Fantasia in 2024.
Credits: Starring: Paul Sun-Hyung Lee, Ethan Hwang, Aaron Schwartz, Richard Wes, Colton Royce, Martin Ortega, Sam Osei, Tyrus Hwang
Written & Directed by: Taylor Ramos & Tony Zhou Produced by: Nach Dudsdeemaytha, Jon Warne, Eric Bizzarri Associate Producer: Rebeca Ortiz Director of Photography: Alfonso Chin Production Designer: Justin Campbell Costume Designer: Asoomii Jay Music by: Sean William Sound Designer: May Guimarães Storyboarded by: Taylor Ramos Edited by: Tony Zhou Casting by: Maureen Webb & Dylan Rogers

item.title: Where Do You Put the Camera?
item.author: Every Frame a Painting
item.content:encoded:
If there’s one question that every filmmaker asks themselves, it’s “Where do I put the camera?” Today, we consider the things that really matter when answering that question.
A Video Essay by Taylor Ramos & Tony Zhou Music by Sean William Additional Stock Music provided by Pond5 BTS footage filmed by Julie Ng
Links to some of the interviews Martin Scorsese & Timothée Chalamet at GQ: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sB_JZLp521g Guillermo del Toro at the DGA: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FjR5bT5YYU0 Greta Gerwig at the BFI London Film Fest: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=joUKN8ezVio
You can check out some merch we made here: https://store.dftba.com/collections/every-frame-a-painting

item.title: The Story of Nosferatu: A Symphony of Horror (1922)
item.author: Layton Eversaul
item.content:encoded:
In this in-depth retrospective, I tell the story behind one of cinema's towering classics: 1922's "Nosferatu: A Symphony of Horror". I dive deep into its development and historical context, its production, release and reception, legal troubles and complex legacy. Directed by F.W. Murnau, with Costumes & Sets by Albin Grau. Starring Max Schreck, Gustav Von Wongenheim, Greta Schröder, and Alexander Granach.
NOW ON PATREON: https://www.patreon.com/LaytonEversaul Twitter: https://twitter.com/LaytonEversaul Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/LaytonReviews Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/laytoneversaul

item.title: When you’re too good for rom-coms - Matthew McConaughey
item.author: FilmStack
item.content:encoded:
The McConaissance: the brief period in Matthew McConaughey’s career where he completely reinvented himself, doing a full 180 from the romcoms that defined his previous decade. With groundbreaking performances in Dallas Buyers Club, Interstellar, and True Detective it taught us a valuable lesson: never underestimate what an actor is truly capable of.
But McConaughey’s sudden career shift didn’t just impress us; it gave everyone whiplash. How did an actor at the top of the romcom world risk everything by stepping away from such lucrative roles? And why, after achieving one of Hollywood’s greatest comebacks, did this golden period fizzle out so quickly? That’s what we’re exploring today. What led McConaughey to even attempt this transformation? How did he execute it so flawlessly? And what happened after the McConaissance ended? This is the story of an actor who just decided to reinvent himself. This is Matthew McConaughey.
Chapters: 00:00 - Intro 00:56 - Rise & Typecasting 05:32 - Why and how? 08:05 - The McConaissance 12:45 - Peak McConaissance 18:05 - How did it end?
Matthew McConaughey. The McConaissance. Dazed and Confused. The Wolf of Wall Street. Leonardo DiCaprio. Contact. The Wedding Planner. How to lose a guy in 10 days. Sahara. Tropic Thunder. The Lincoln Lawyer. Bernie. Killer Joe. Dallas Buyers Club. Magic Mike. Mud. Interstellar. Rust Cohle. True Detective. The Dark Tower. Sing. Kubo and the Two Strings. Fool's Gold. Best Modern Actor. Martin Scorcese. Christopher Nolan.
If you like this video don't forget to leave a like, and if you're interested in videos about movies and the film industry in general, make sure to subscribe to FilmStack for more great content.
If you have any other ideas for videos, leave a comment and I might make a video with your idea.
Patreon: https://patreon.com/FilmStack Twitter: https://twitter.com/realFilmStack Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/realfilmstack/
#matthewmcconaughey #dedication #bestactor

item.title: Half in the Bag: Trash Dracula Look Like Boat
item.author: RedLetterMedia
item.content:encoded:
Mike and Jay finally went outside in the bitter Wisconsin winter to go to a movie theater, where they proceeded to have popcorn chewed right behind their heads for 90 minutes by an oblivious caveman who never learned to chew with his mouth shut.
They also saw Nosferatu.

Based on the Pulitzer Prize-winning novel by Colson Whitehead, "Nickel Boys" chronicles the powerful friendship between two young African-American men navigating the harrowing trials of reform school together in Florida.

item.title: Half in the Bag: 2024 Stragglers
item.author: RedLetterMedia
item.content:encoded:
Happy New Year! Jay and Mike catch up on some the remaining artistically ambitious disasters from earlier in the year that they haven't seen until now such as Megalopolis and Joker 2: Joker Goes Hawaiian.
(00:00) Intro (02:51) The Substance (09:20) Smile 2 (16:24) Youtuber Movies (18:58) Anora (22:35) Megalopolis (32:35) Joker: Folie à Deux

Chapters: 0:00 Intro 0:59 Gone Huntin’ 1:16 PEZ disagreement 1:55 Passing Thought 3:10 Alien Frisbee 3:33 Thanks Sandy 4:15 Scout Leader 5:06 Smokey’s Revenge 6:00 The Shack 7:12 Car Owner’s Worst Nightmare 7:55 The Bar 8:34 Not Waiting 9:04 The Alien Reveal 10:04 Relief? 10:46 Back to the Shack 11:36 The Alien 12:05 Shooting At Night 14:00 The Score 14:23 What I Didn’t Like 14:48 Predator 15:24 Distribution 15:49 Final Thoughts 16:40 End Skit / Outtakes / Credits
#predator #alien #horrormovies
